task: Fixed four critical bugs in transcript capture, token truncation, plan preservation, and max_tokens enforcement that were causing handover LLMs to lose context, and confirmed output-side token limits are correctly implemented via API parameters.
discussion_summary: The askr system had interconnected bugs in checkpoint.py's _build_transcript_text() that were stripping tool input context and blindly truncating transcript/handover text at arbitrary character counts ([:400], [:300], [:80], [:4000]), causing plan definitions and tool inputs to be severed before the handover LLM ever saw them. This session fixed all four issues: (1) capturing actual tool input in transcript text instead of collapsing to bare tool names, (2) removing all blind character-count truncation caps and sending full content, (3) preserving plan tool-call entries in the transcript window regardless of position while maintaining recency filtering for other entries, and (4) surfacing max_tokens truncation via stop_reason checking in the Claude API client instead of failing silently. The user also rejected any manual post-generation truncation of LLM outputs, requiring instead that token limits be enforced via max_tokens parameter on API calls; investigation confirmed this is already correctly implemented.

accomplishments.what: Removed blind character-count truncation ([:400], [:300], [:80], [:4000]) from checkpoint.py _build_transcript_text and existing_json embedding; now sends full content to handover LLM
Captured actual tool input for plan tool-calls (TaskCreate, TaskUpdate) in transcript text instead of collapsing to bare TOOL: {name}
Replaced blind entries[-60:] recency slice in read_transcript with filter that always retains plan-defining tool calls plus recency window for other entries
Added stop_reason checking in clients/claude.py _post_messages/call_claude to detect and log max_tokens truncation explicitly instead of failing silently
All 188 tests passing after changes; verified _is_plan_entry() correctly identifies TaskCreate/TaskUpdate tool calls

next_actions.action: Fix quota-trigger to wait for Stop-hook signal before launching companion session, matching _open_companion_session_for_trigger's context-trigger behavior (TaskId 5)
next_actions.why: Quota path in lifecycle.py _execute_trigger currently launches companion sessions without waiting for turn-completion signal, creating race conditions and context loss; context-trigger already has correct pattern

next_actions.action: Verify that stage-plan context is preserved through autonomous handover jobs (e.g., git save, test runner) that run after context-switch stops
next_actions.why: User confirmed that session continuity problem extends to autonomous jobs running between user interactions; when multi-step stage plan is interrupted mid-implementation, autonomous job has no knowledge of remaining stages

next_actions.action: Review lifecycle.py code-path reuse across trigger types (quota, context, idle) to eliminate semantic mismatches that caused idle trigger to be misannounced as quota emergency
next_actions.why: Root cause of false inactivity alerts and unwanted session auto-launches; idle semantics (user absence) differ fundamentally from quota/context semantics (resource exhaustion)

decisions.decision: Idle trigger uses dedicated _checkpoint_idle_inactivity() function, not generic _execute_trigger() shared with quota/context triggers
decisions.reason: Idle semantics (user absence from app) differ fundamentally from quota/context semantics (resource exhaustion); code-path reuse caused false announcements and unwanted session launches

decisions.decision: Voice broadcast fires only on emergency conditions (quota/context at 90%+), never per-turn
decisions.reason: Per-turn announcements create spam and false urgency; emergencies are rare and warrant interruption

decisions.decision: ask CLI uses separate API key, not Claude Code's OAuth token
decisions.reason: Keeps CLI and Claude Code authentication domains separate; prevents quota/cost confusion between interactive and CLI usage

decisions.decision: Token limits must be enforced via max_tokens parameter on API calls, not via post-generation truncation
decisions.reason: Mindless truncation after generation wastes compute, loses semantic structure, and breaks LLM output integrity; enforcement at call time is cleaner and more predictable

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Manual post-generation truncation of LLM outputs to fit context windows
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: Rejected; requires instead that token limits be enforced via max_tokens parameter on API calls

relational_files.why: Contains _execute_trigger() and _open_companion_session_for_trigger() that need alignment on quota-trigger wait behavior
Validates checkpoint.py changes; all 188 tests passing
Validates checkpoint.py changes; all 188 tests passing
